title: Solicited call under Social Development Partnerships Program – Disability Component – Disability Inclusion Action Plan: Closed - Canada.ca
content:
## Solicited call under Social Development Partnerships Program – Disability Component – Disability Inclusion Action Plan: Closed
- Application period Closed
Current status: Closed
Applicants must not start their proposed projects until notified by Employment and Social Development Canada (ESDC) of approval and an agreement has been signed between the organization and a representative of ESDC.
Funding is limited and subject to budget considerations of ESDC and allocation of funds by Parliament.
Learn more about other funding opportunities
The application period ran from August 5, 2021 to September 17, 2021 at 5:00 p.m. Eastern Time (ET).
We expect that projects will begin in fall 2021.
Note: Only specific organizations can apply.
The Social Development Partnerships Program -  Disability Component (SDPP-D) aims to improve the participation and integration  of persons with disabilities in all aspects of Canadian society with respect to  social inclusion. Canadians with disabilities can face unique personal, social  and economic barriers to participation. As a result, they may experience a  diminished quality of life, with limited ability to participate in the  workplace or to contribute to their communities.
Projects funded under this program will support a  wide range of initiatives that address social issues and barriers experienced  by persons with disabilities.
This  solicited process will support disability stakeholder-led engagement activities  on the Disability Inclusion Action Plan that aims to:
- improve  the social and economic inclusion of Canadians with disabilities
- reduce  poverty among Canadians with disabilities
- contribute  to the realization of a barrier-free Canada by 2040
- improve  access to federal programs and services for persons with disabilities and  ensure that disability inclusion is considered in all Government programs,  policies and services, and
- foster a  culture of inclusion and a shift away from attitudes of ableism and discrimination
In result of this process, one partnership-based  agreement will be funded.
The successful project will begin in fall 2021  and end by March 31, 2023.
Find  information on funded grants and contribution projects in proactive disclosure
This is a solicited funding process and only invited  organizations can submit a proposal.
If you have questions, please email  us at accessible-canada@hrsdc-rhdcc.gc.ca before September 17, 2021 at 12:00 pm Eastern time.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
